mission.title: oras: `oras cp` fails if `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name` contains a full reference
mission.description: `oras cp` fails if `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name` contains a full reference. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify oras troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your oras deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras -n oras --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review oras configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant oras configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras
kubectl get configmap -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oras
```
### What happened in your environment?

We use OCI images to package eBPF programs in [Inspektor Gadget](https://github.com/inspektor-gadget/inspektor-gadget/).

steps.title: Apply the fix for `oras cp` fails if `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name`…
steps.description:
The oci-layout can't be used when the image reference contains a slash ( see issue 1505). This PR introduces a new oci-layout-path that explicitly receives the path of the oci layout fixing the parsing ambiguity.

*(optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will
```yaml
$ oras cp --from-oci-layout ./mygadget.tar.folder:ghcr.io/inspektor-gadget/gadget/mygadget:latest localhost:5000/foo:latest --debug
Error: invalid argument "./mygadget.tar.folder:ghcr.io/inspektor-gadget/gadget/mygadget:latest": failed to find path "./mygadget.tar.folder:ghcr.io/inspektor-gadget/gadget/mygadget": stat ./mygadget.tar.folder:ghcr.io/inspektor-gadget/gadget/mygadget: no such file or directory
```

steps.title: Confirm `oras cp` fails if `org.opencontainers.image.ref.n… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras -n oras --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n oras --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The oci-layout can't be used when the image reference contains a slash ( see issue 1505). This PR introduces a new oci-layout-path that explicitly receives the path of the oci layout fixing the parsing ambiguity.

*(optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will close the issue(s) when PR gets merged)*:
- [X]  Does the affected code have corresponding tests, e.g.
codeSnippets:
